description: Triple-negative breast cancer (ER-, PR-, HER2-). PARP inhibitors are key in BRCA-mutated patients; immunotherapy is relevant in PD-L1-positive disease.

interactionRules.name: BRCA1 + BRCA2 co-mutation strengthens HR-deficient phenotype
interactionRules.description: Co-mutation of BRCA1 and BRCA2 produces a more profound homologous recombination deficiency, strengthening the rationale for PARP inhibitors and platinum chemotherapy.

effects.reason: Compounded HR deficiency increases predicted PARP inhibitor sensitivity.
Compounded HR deficiency increases predicted PARP inhibitor sensitivity.
Compounded HR deficiency increases predicted platinum sensitivity.

interactionRules.name: PD-L1 expression with BRCA mutation suggests combination strategy
interactionRules.description: Concurrent BRCA mutation and PD-L1 expression supports a PARP + immunotherapy combination strategy, an active area of clinical investigation.

effects.reason: BRCA-deficient tumors may have higher neoantigen burden, augmenting immunotherapy response.
